- [ ] 2.5 Add environment variable to .env
  - Files:
    - `.env` (modify - add ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE)
    - `.env.example` (modify if exists - add comment)
  - Add ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=true for test environment
  - Add comment explaining feature flag purpose
  - Purpose: Enable test endpoints in development/test environments
  - _Leverage: None (environment configuration)_
  - _Requirements: Requirement 1 (Feature Flag Control)_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec test-data-endpoint, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: DevOps Engineer | Task: Add ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=true to root .env file with comment explaining it enables internal test endpoints, update .env.example if it exists with ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=false and security warning | Restrictions: Set to 'true' in .env (for development), set to 'false' in .env.example (safe default for production), include clear comment about purpose and security implications | Success: Environment variable is added to both files with appropriate values and comments, developers understand when to enable/disable flag | Instructions: After completing implementation, mark this task as completed in tasks.md by changing `- [ ]` to `- [x]`_
